title: Date and time — Representations for information interchange
note: Format strings use strftime-style tokens where illustrative; regex patterns are partial validators.

formats.name: Extended complete date
formats.notes: Preferred form. Unambiguous in all locales.

formats.name: Basic complete date
formats.notes: Compact form; must not be confused with other 8-digit codes.

formats.name: Year and month (extended)
formats.notes: Day component omitted. Not to be confused with YYYYMM (basic form).

formats.name: Week date (extended)
formats.notes: W = literal, ww = week number (01–53), D = day of week (1=Mon … 7=Sun).

time.description: Time-of-day representations. Separator T is required when combined with a date.

formats.name: Extended time with fractional seconds
formats.notes: Decimal fraction of seconds. The comma (,) is also allowed as decimal sign per the standard.

timezone.description: Time zone designators appended to time or datetime values.

datetime.description: Combined date and time. The date and time components are separated by the letter T.

formats.name: Extended datetime, UTC
formats.notes: Most common serialisation format. Used by JSON Schema, OpenAPI, RFC 3339.

formats.name: Extended datetime with offset
formats.notes: Explicit UTC offset preserves the local time context.

formats.name: Extended datetime with fractional seconds, UTC
formats.notes: Millisecond precision. Used by JavaScript's Date.toISOString().

formats.name: Basic datetime
formats.notes: Compact form; avoid in contexts where it may be ambiguous.

duration.description: Representation of a time span. Starts with the letter P (period).

examples.meaning: 1 year, 2 months, 3 days, 4 hours, 5 minutes, 6 seconds
Alternative week form using date designators

duration.notes: T separates date and time components inside a duration. Components may be omitted if zero. At least one component must be present.

interval.description: Time interval between two points in time or of a fixed duration.

formats.name: Duration only
formats.notes: When the endpoints can be inferred from context.

recurring_interval.description: Repeating time interval. Prefixed with Rn/ where n is the recurrence count (omit n for unbounded).

ordering_rules: Dates must use a four-digit year (negative years must be agreed out-of-band).
The T separator between date and time is mandatory in combined representations.
Always include a timezone designator in datetimes meant for interchange.
Prefer the extended form (with hyphens/colons) for human-readable contexts.
Prefer the basic form only in filenames, headers, or compact protocols.
The comma decimal separator (e.g. 14:30:00,5) is technically valid but the period is preferred.
